user_symptom: Customers will experience increased latency. Query (and other) jobs will timeout, or fail.

Almost all services recovered within the initial 33 minutes of the incident; exceptions are outlined in the detailed impact section below. Affected customers experienced elevated error rates and latency when connecting to Google APIs.
Affected customers in Europe were unable to load the console, or experienced timeouts.
Increased error rate

Customers may have experienced errors or incomplete monitoring data. Missing precomputed data from between 11:00 PT and 12:45 PT is expected, but can still be viewed via raw query. Customers may have also experienced false alerts during the impact window based on the underlying monitoring data.
We are experiencing network latency in us-central1 and us-east1
Symptoms: APIs, Credentials, Monitoring, IAM and several other pages on the Cloud console are loading slowly and may end up as a loading failure.
We are experiencing an issue with Cloud SQL.
customers experiencing elevated rates of packet loss
Slow or stuck operations for zonal PD-Standard devices in us-west1-b
Affected customers in Europe were unable to load the console, or experienced timeouts.

service_name: Google Workspace and Google Cloud Platform (GCP)
user_symptom: Affected customers encountered error 500 when accessing various services like Calendar, Tasks, Gmail, Drive, Docs, Meet, Keep, Jamboard, Chat, Admin Console, and Groups.
Significant packet loss on Cloud Interconnect in europe-west2 for some customers.
Queries exhibited higher than normal latency. Customers were unable to fully utilize their reserved slots.
Affected customers may have experienced delayed log entries in Log Explorer or other export destinations for the duration of the incident.
Google Cloud customers using VMWare Engine experienced connectivity issues while using vSphere, vCenter and NSX in us-central1a zone for a duration of 6 hours, 23 minutes.
Logs-based metrics data points may be missing or delayed
Logs won't be available, high latency when reading data.
Issues with seeing monitoring metric data especially delivery_latency_health_score. Other metrics data may also be affected.

Customers using CNAME chasing may experience an issue with Cloud DNS. Querying for a record defined in one private zone that points to a record in another private zone does not return the final answer.
new connections to Cloud Pub/Sub experienced high latency leading to timeouts and elevated error rates for a duration of 95 minutes
HTTP load balancing returning error for some users in Europe
Users experiencing failure with file uploads.

service_name: Google Cloud Networking and Google Cloud DNS, Service Directory
user_symptom: Customers might experience errors with config changes, Cloud Armor rules, Appengine flex deployments, Traffic Director configs, and VM instance group health checks.
Customer may experience replication failure on the new CSA rollout on replica

Users experienced increased packet loss and elevated latency, causing delays and timeouts for services like Cloud Load Balancing, Cloud Interconnect, Virtual Private Cloud (VPC), Cloud NAT, and Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE).
Customers may observe deployment failures and timeout errors.

service_name: Google Cloud Identity and Access Management (IAM)
user_symptom: Affected customers would have experienced increased error rates when creating buckets in the Asia multi-region.
Affected customers would have been unable to create new clusters in the affected regions.
Customers might have experienced increased errors from apps or functions that use the Secrets Manager.
Composer 2 environments upgraded to newer Composer versions might experience problems with autoscaling.

Customers using Cloud Load Balancing would have experienced increased packet loss up to 60% for egress traffic from Google to the Middle East and increased network latency between our Europe and Asia regions.
Customer configs propagation depending on the dos_cloud configs got delayed. Some impacted customers would not be able to make Cloud Armor config changes.
Cloud Function 2nd Generation deletes are erroring out in multiple regions.
Increase in failure rate for SQLServer Instance Creation
Customers experienced a cloud networking disruption
Affected customers would not have been able to create new repositories in the Asia multi-region. Customers would have received a failure message of 'Request contains an invalid argument.'

Affected customers in the Asia multi-region were not able to create Composer environments, receiving errors indicating either an issue with AppEngine or Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) cluster creation.

Affected customers would have experienced increased error rates when creating buckets in the Asia multi-region. GKE uses Google Cloud multi-regional buckets internally, so regional GKE customers throughout Asia Pacific were affected.
Affected customers would have been unable to create new clusters in the affected regions.
Customers encountered error 429 when listing Cloud Filestore instances using API, gcloud CLI, and Google Cloud Console UI.
Customers may experience latency and errors with Composer creation.
Customers may be missing data in Cloud Monitoring for specific metrics related to Google Cloud Bigtable.
Customers using the Secrets Manager are experiencing increased errors.

service_name: Google Cloud Functions (GCFv2) and Cloud Run API

Customers may see older jobs (finished more than 180 days ago) and jobs which are currently running disappear from the Console and API output. Jobs which are currently running will see data plane errors if the control plane job resource is deleted.
